title: Gorgon

{{Quote|Daala, I am giving you the power to turn any planet to slag.|[[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]|<ref name="Jedi Search" />}}
The '''''Gorgon''''' was an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] and the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]]. Constructed by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] over [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] along with its sister [[Starship/Legends|ships]] [[Hydra (Imperial I-class)|''Hydra'']], [[Basilisk (Star Destroyer)|''Basilisk'']], and ''[[Manticore]]'', these [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] were part of the [[Maw fleet]], stationed at [[Maw Installation]], a secret [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] research facility under Admiral Daala's command. With her second-in-command, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Kratas]], Daala drilled the crew of the ''Gorgon'' until the vessel became, in Daala's opinion, one of the best performing Star Destroyers in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Fleet]]. Daala and the ''Gorgon'' were stationed at Maw Installation without any outside contact for a decade before a [[Endor (500-X shuttle)|lone]] [[500-X shuttle]] entered [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] after departing from [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] and was captured by the ''Gorgon''{{'s}} [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]]. From the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle's]] crew, Daala learned that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] was near defeat following [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and took it upon herself to launch a [[Daala campaign|guerrilla war]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].

Intent on destroying the New Republic, Daala engaged the opposition in battles such as the [[attack on Mon Calamari]], where she was soundly defeated, and soon forced to retreat. Daala fled back to the Maw with the ''Gorgon'', her lone remaining Star Destroyer, where she [[Battle of the Maw|encountered]] New Republic forces. In an effort to prevent the New Republic from gaining top-secret Imperial data, the ''Gorgon'' destroyed the Installation by firing upon its unstable [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]]. The New Republic believed that Daala had crashed the ship into the Installation in a suicide effort, although the ''Gorgon'' had actually fired upon the facility with its [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] before jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] under cover of the explosion. The irreparably damaged ''Gorgon'' jumped to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. There, Daala met up with the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]], highly-ranked officials who had assumed control of what was left of the Empire. The Star Destroyer was later dismantled by the warlords for use in the construction of newer vessels.

==Description==
{{Quote|I had forgotten how sleek and maneuverable a Star Destroyer could be, after all the damage we suffered on the ''Gorgon''.|[[Kratas]]|<ref name="Darksaber">[[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']]</ref>}}
The ''Gorgon'' was 1,600 meters long, the standard length for an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]]. Heavily armed, with sixty [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries and sixty [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], [[Natasi Daala]] considered her vessel powerful enough to lay an entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to waste.<ref name="DA" /> The [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] also possessed ten [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors and a sizable [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]]. For support craft, the ''Gorgon'' was capable of carrying up to six full [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and two [[Gamma-class assault shuttle/Legends|''Gamma''-class assault shuttles]], one of which was the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Edict (Gamma-class assault shuttle)|''Edict'']], used personally by Daala, who often [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] it herself. The ''Gorgon'' was unusual in that it possessed a smaller complement of assault and support craft than was standard for a Star Destroyer.<ref name="Jedi Search" /> Similarly, the ''Gorgon'' did not carry any [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]].<ref name="DA" /> The Star Destroyer also held twenty [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] and thirty [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers for ground warfare. The ''Gorgon'' was crewed by less than 45,000 men, the standard crew. Daala once described her ship as being run by a skeleton crew.<ref name="Jedi Search" /> Finally, the ''Gorgon'' had been designed with a special [[Emperor's throne room|throne room]] aboard for explicit use by the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], in accordance with all ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers.<ref name="Guide to SW">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref>

==History==
===Construction and deployment===
{{Quote|Those were our defenses. Maw Installation depended entirely on Admiral Daala's fleet.|[[Qwi Xux]]|<ref name="CotF" />}}
The ''Gorgon'' was commissioned by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] and constructed over [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] alongside three other [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]]: the [[Hydra (Imperial I-class)|''Hydra'']], the [[Basilisk (Star Destroyer)|''Basilisk'']], and the ''[[Manticore]]''. Tarkin intended these vessels to guard his secret military research facility, [[Maw Installation]], where [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] such as the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], [[Sun Crusher]] and the [[World Devastator]]s were designed. The ''Gorgon'' and the rest of the [[Maw fleet]] were put under the direct command of Tarkin's mistress, Natasi Daala, the first [[Gender/Legends|female]] to become an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Daala personally oversaw the construction of the ''Gorgon'', which would serve as her [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], from its early stages and was bent on turning it into a model of Star Destroyer perfection.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

Tarkin placed the Maw fleet in a [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] island at the center of [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]], a cluster of [[Black hole/Legends|black holes]], with no outside contact and a strict mandate to remain and guard the facility at all costs. Known only to Tarkin and the Emperor, the Maw Installation was unknown to the outside [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and the Empire at large.<ref name="Jedi Search" /> Therefore, Tarkin chose individuals with no ties to the outside galaxy for Daala's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], so that they would not be missed from isolation in the Maw, and no one would raise questions. He and Daala also took into consideration the skill of the crewers they picked for service on the ''Gorgon'', choosing those whom they believed to be highly qualified.<ref name="JAS">''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]''</ref> However, Daala feared that a lack of active service would cause her crew's skills to deteriorate. This, and the fear that her crew would resent being commanded by a woman, led her to drill her crew constantly, and she executed several officers to show her crew that she would stop at nothing to keep them in line. In the depths of the Maw, the ''Gorgon'' sat at full battle readiness, waiting for a chance to prove itself, its crew constantly put on edge by its relentless commander and her second-in-command, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Kratas]]. But as the crew slowly grew bored over the next decade, despite their commander, they yearned for a chance to break free of the Maw and return to active duty.<ref name="Jedi Search" /> Having exhausted the entertainment libraries and with no opportunities to transfer to a new station or advance in rank, discontent was rife. Furthermore, with a ten-year-long deployment, the crew began to age, with the reflexes of some of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] pilots beginning to dull as they moved beyond the typical age threshold for those positions.<ref name="JAS" />

===Leaving the Maw===
{{Quote|The Empire lies wounded and vulnerable on the outside, and we can't just sit here and wait. We have four Star Destroyers, a full fleet the Rebellion knows nothing about.|[[Natasi Daala]]|<ref name="Jedi Search" />}}
The ''Gorgon''{{'s}} departure from the Maw came when a lone [[500-X shuttle]], [[Endor (500-X shuttle)|''Endor'']], entered the Maw. When its crew, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] personnel [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Kyp Durron]], failed to provide the correct security code, the ''Gorgon'' used its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors to pull the shuttle into one of its [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bays. Because of the inability to transmit signals through the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]] from the Maw due to the ionized gases around the black holes, the crew of the ''Gorgon'', especially Daala, was unprepared for the news the outsiders brought with them. While the ''Gorgon'' was in isolation, Grand Moff Tarkin had [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|died]] on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], and Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] had both [[Battle of Endor/Legends|perished]] on a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]]. Due to the latter two deaths, the Empire was tearing itself apart in a [[Imperial Civil War|civil war]], while the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] had transitioned into the New Republic and had become a dominant power in the galaxy.<ref name="Jedi Search" />

[[File:Suncrusher-smash.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The [[Hydra (Imperial I-class)|''Hydra'']] is rammed by the [[Sun Crusher]].]]
Dumbstruck by the news, Daala decided that since Tarkin was dead, she was no longer held down by his orders and could fight her own guerrilla war against the New Republic. Imprisoning the shuttle's crew and declaring to the Maw [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] that she was leaving, Daala scrambled her forces and prepared to depart. During the departure preparations, the prisoners escaped with the Installation's latest project, the [[Sun Crusher]] superweapon, aided by Maw scientist [[Qwi Xux]].<ref name="Jedi Search" />

The four Star Destroyers scrambled to stop the escapees from leaving the Maw. However, since the Sun Crusher's hull was practically invulnerable to harm, the most the fleet could do was physically smother the superweapon with TIE Fighters. Meanwhile, the ''Hydra'' positioned itself to block the Sun Crusher from escaping through the only direct route out of the Maw. Undaunted, the Sun Crusher sped out of the TIE Fighter swarm and through the ''Hydra''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. The crippled Star Destroyer was drawn in by the gravitational force of a nearby black hole. The way cleared, the Sun Crusher fled into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="Jedi Search" />

Vowing that the Sun Crusher had to be recovered at all costs, Daala ordered the ''Gorgon'' and its two remaining sister ships to follow the superweapon out of the Maw. The ''Gorgon'' caught up with its quarry as it exited the Maw but [[Skirmish in the Maw|ran straight]] into the [[Doole's defense fleet|mercenary fleet]] of [[Moruth Doole]], which was looking for friends of Han Solo, who had been searching for him and Chewbacca since their disappearance from Doole's [[Prison/Legends|prison]] on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] in the ''Endor''. The confused fleet fired on the imposing Star Destroyers, but their shots failed to significantly harm the Imperial warships. Daala's fleet quickly mopped up the rag-tag force, albeit with losses, and the ''Gorgon'' sustained minimal damage.<ref name="Jedi Search" /> With the latest unexpected threat out of her way, Daala ordered the ''Gorgon'' to conduct a [[Daala campaign|campaign]] of piracy on New Republic shipping.<ref name="DA" />

===War against the New Republic===
{{Quote|We have three Star Destroyers. That's enough to wipe out civilizations on any number of worlds. We must hit fast and run fast. We will continue to pound the Rebels for as long as we can.|Natasi Daala|<ref name="DA" />}}
Their first merchant target was a small [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. After firing several shots at the ship before it surrendered, the ''Gorgon'' pulled the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] into its hangar. From the captured [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[T'nun Bdu]], Daala learned that the vessel was delivering relief supplies to a "peaceful" colony on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. The ''Gorgon''{{'s}} crew, however, found a large cache of high-grade weapons and communications equipment aboard. Daala had [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] placed among the corvette's engines, and then ordered the ship to be released. After it sent out a distress call, which Daala was sure would distract the New Republic, the thermal detonators were triggered, destroying the vessel. The ''Gorgon'' then set course for the colony on Dantooine.<ref name="DA" />

Despite Dantooine being a vital communications outpost, the ''Gorgon''{{'s}} sensors showed that the small colony possessed few means of defense. Nevertheless, the ''Gorgon'' launched several [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] with which to [[Attack on Dantooine (Galactic Civil War)|obliterate]] the colonists. The operation succeeded and, recalling her men to the ''Gorgon'', Daala set her eyes on the [[Mon Calamari Orbital Shipyards|vital shipyards]] of the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] world of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]].<ref name="DA" />

[[File:MonCalamariBombingRun.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The [[Attack on Mon Calamari|Battle of Mon Calamari]].]]
Daala laid out what she considered a [[Attack on Mon Calamari|fool-proof plan]], based on a tactic created by Tarkin himself. The battle would start with the ''Gorgon'' and ''Basilisk'' opening fire and launching an attack on the floating [[City/Legends|cities]] of the Mon Calamari, thus drawing forces away from the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], while the ''Manticore'' would come out from the shadow of the planet's [[Dac's Moon|moon]] and destroy the helpless starship construction facilities. The plan was going well until a former [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] of Tarkin's, retired [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Defense Fleet]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], recognized the tactic and, using a Mon Calamari code, remotely took control of the half-finished [[MC90 Star Cruiser]] ''[[Startide]]'' and used it to crash into the ''Manticore''. Accepting her first major defeat in her guerrilla offensive, Daala's two remaining Star Destroyers fled to the safety of the [[Cauldron Nebula/Legends|Cauldron Nebula]].<ref name="DA" />

While there, Daala made plans to use ''Basilisk'' in a suicide crash attack on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet of the New Republic. However, just prior to the launch of the assault on Coruscant, Kyp Durron, one of the escaped Maw prisoners, arrived with the Sun Crusher and attacked the ''Gorgon''. Daala rushed to reclaim her lost prize, but she soon realized that it was just a stall tactic, and Durron had already instigated a [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in several of the [[Nebula/Legends|nebula's]] [[Star/Legends|stars]]. Daala ordered the ''Gorgon'' and the ''Basilisk'' to make an emergency hyperspace jump. As the burst of energy radiated out from the supernova explosions, the ''Gorgon'' barely escaped the nebula. The ''Basilisk'' was not so lucky, however, and it was incinerated.<ref name="DA" />

===Final battle===
{{Quote|Red alert, an Imperial Star Destroyer has entered the vicinity. Red alert! Prepare for attack.|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], from Maw Installation's command room|<ref name="CotF" />}}
With the ''Gorgon'' severely damaged by the [[destruction of the Cauldron Nebula]], Daala realized that she should have never abandoned her station at the Maw, and in disobeying her mandate, she had caused the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of many of her best soldiers. She then commanded the ''Gorgon'' to retreat to Maw Installation, where she realized two things. First, she saw that the [[Death Star prototype]], a semi-permanent fixture around the installation, was missing. Second, a small New Republic fleet was now [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] comprising the installation. Realizing that the New Republic had seized Maw Installation, Daala vowed to [[Battle of the Maw|destroy]] the facility, or die trying. The ''Gorgon'' unleashed a punishing turbolaser volley on the [[Space station/Legends|station]] from its remaining gun emplacements.<ref name="CotF" />

The New Republic fleet scrambled to meet the ''Gorgon''. Soon, to add to the mounting confusion, the Death Star prototype, under the command of Maw Installation administrator [[Tol Sivron]], entered the Maw. Despite the fact that Sivron would not fire the prototype's [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]], the looming threat of his weapon made the New Republic frantic. Daala took advantage of the situation by using the ''Gorgon''{{'s}} [[Computer/Legends|computers]] to extract information from Maw Installation's archives. With the vital schematics in hand, she ordered the ''Gorgon'' to fire on the installation's by-now unstable [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]].<ref name="CotF" /> As a bright explosion filled the [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]] of the New Republic ships, the ''Gorgon'' made its escape and fled to the remnants of the Empire in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], seeking refuge. However, the New Republic believed that Daala had crashed the ''Gorgon'' into the facility in a suicidal effort to prevent them from gaining top-secret information contained in the Installation. It was not until a [[12 ABY/Legends|year later]] that the New Republic would learn she had survived.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Damaged nearly beyond repair, the ''Gorgon'' was seized by the various Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who had domains in the Deep Core and dismantled using the parts for construction of other [[Warship/Legends|warships]]. Daala's remaining crew was scattered across the Deep Core. Daala herself, along with Kratas, roamed from warlord to warlord in an attempt to [[Imperial Reunification|unify]] the fractured Empire, an effort that ultimately succeeded in gaining Daala command of an [[United Warlord Fleets|unified Imperial war machine]].<ref name="Darksaber" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Gorgon'' was one of four Star Destroyers created by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] for the [[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|''The Jedi Academy Trilogy'']], and it recurred throughout the series as Admiral Daala's flagship.<ref name="Jedi Search" /><ref name="DA" /><ref name="CotF" /> The ''Gorgon'' was mentioned briefly in the novel [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']], where its final fate was explained.<ref name="Darksaber" />
